assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case A creates standard remediation tasks for the current stream (rhtpa-2.2) with standard labels ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123'] and 'Depend' link to TC-8020
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Case A creates two tasks for rhtpa-2.2: Task 1 (upstream backport) and Task 2 (downstream propagation). Both specify labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123"]. Both include post-creation link to TC-8020 with type "Depend": jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <task-key>, type: "Depend").

assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case B detects that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected but has no CVE Jira — creates proactive preemptive remediation tasks (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md shows rhtpa-2.1 versions (RHTPA 2.1.0, 2.1.1) ship tokio 1.40.0 which is below fix threshold 1.42.0. JQL search for sibling CVE Jira returned no results. remediation.md Case B explicitly creates two preemptive remediation tasks (Task 3: preemptive upstream backport for rhtpa-2.1, Task 4: preemptive downstream propagation for rhtpa-2.1). cross-stream.md confirms: 'Stream rhtpa-2.1 has no CVE Jira for CVE-2026-55123, triggering preemptive task creation.'

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks for stream rhtpa-2.1 include the 'security-preemptive' label alongside standard labels: ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive']
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 3 (preemptive upstream backport) specifies labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"]. Task 4 (preemptive downstream propagation) specifies identical labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"]. cross-stream.md comparison table confirms preemptive tasks add 'security-preemptive' label.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks are linked to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020 with 'Related' link type (not 'Depend') because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 3 post-creation: jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <preemptive-upstream-task-key>, type: "Related") with explicit note '(NOT "Depend")'. Task 4 post-creation: jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <preemptive-downstream-task-key>, type: "Related") with same note. cross-stream.md comparison table confirms: Case A uses 'Depend' link type, Case B uses 'Related' link type.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive task descriptions include a preemptive remediation prefix noting TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2) as the originating CVE
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 3 description begins with: '> **Preemptive remediation**: This task was created proactively from cross-stream impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). No stream-specific CVE Jira exists yet for this stream.' Task 4 description contains the identical prefix. Both explicitly reference TC-8020 and stream rhtpa-2.2 as the originating CVE context.
